UserAccount.title: UserAccount
UserAccount.description: Represents a user's account for authentication and system access to Nebulabs WebOS. (Security Best Practice: Passwords and sensitive authentication data are handled by an external system and not stored here.)

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserAccount entity.

username.description: A unique username chosen by the user for login and display.

email.description: A unique email address associated with the user account.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was last updated.

UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores additional user-specific information not directly related to authentication or core settings, but enhancing the user's profile.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

userAccountId.description: Reference to the UserAccount this profile belongs to. (Relationship: UserAccount 1:1 UserProfile)

displayName.description: The user's preferred display name, which may differ from their username.

avatarUrl.description: URL to the user's profile avatar image.

lastLoginAt.description: Timestamp of the user's last successful login.

UserSetting.title: UserSetting
UserSetting.description: Stores individual user preferences and configurations for the Nebulabs WebOS, such as theme choices, desktop background, and other UI parameters.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserSetting entity.

userAccountId.description: Reference to the UserAccount this setting belongs to. (Relationship: UserAccount 1:N UserSetting)

key.description: The unique key identifying the specific setting (e.g., 'themeName', 'desktopBackgroundColor').

value.description: The value associated with the setting key. This can be a simple string or a JSON stringified object for complex settings.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the setting was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the setting was last updated.

Application.title: Application
Application.description: Defines an application available in the Nebulabs OS App Store or bundled with the OS.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Application entity.

name.description: The display name of the application (e.g., 'Google Docs', 'Terminal').

description.description: A brief description of what the application does.

iconUrl.description: URL to the application's icon image.

version.description: The current version of the application.

publisher.description: The entity or company that published the application.

category.description: The category the application belongs to (e.g., 'Productivity', 'System Tools', 'Utilities').

isBundledGoogleApp.description: Indicates if the application is a pre-integrated Google product.

launchCommand.description: A command or identifier used by the OS to launch the application.

UserInstalledApplication.title: UserInstalledApplication
UserInstalledApplication.description: Records which applications a specific user has installed or has access to, including layout preferences on their virtual desktop.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserInstalledApplication entity.

userAccountId.description: Reference to the UserAccount that installed this application. (Relationship: UserAccount 1:N UserInstalledApplication)

applicationId.description: Reference to the Application definition from the App Store. (Relationship: Application 1:N UserInstalledApplication)

installedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the application was installed by the user.

lastUsedAt.description: Timestamp of the last time the user launched or interacted with this application.

isPinnedToTaskbar.description: Indicates if the application's icon is pinned to the user's taskbar.

desktopIconPositionX.description: The X-coordinate for the application's desktop icon position.

desktopIconPositionY.description: The Y-coordinate for the application's desktop icon position.

UserFolder.title: UserFolder
UserFolder.description: Represents a folder within a user's virtual file system, allowing for hierarchical organization of files and other folders.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserFolder entity.

userAccountId.description: Reference to the UserAccount that owns this folder. (Relationship: UserAccount 1:N UserFolder)

parentFolderId.description: Reference to the parent folder's ID, if this folder is nested. Null for root-level folders. (Relationship: UserFolder 1:N UserFolder (Self-referencing))

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the folder was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the folder was last updated.

UserFile.title: UserFile
UserFile.description: Represents a file stored within a user's virtual file system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserFile entity.

userAccountId.description: Reference to the UserAccount that owns this file. (Relationship: UserAccount 1:N UserFile)

folderId.description: Reference to the UserFolder where this file is located. (Relationship: UserFolder 1:N UserFile)

name.description: The name of the file, including its extension.

mimeType.description: The MIME type of the file (e.g., 'text/plain', 'image/png', 'application/vnd.google-docs').

sizeBytes.description: The size of the file in bytes.

storagePath.description: An internal path or reference to the actual storage location of the file's content.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the file was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the file was last updated.

definition.description: Stores core user account information. The document ID (`{userId}`) corresponds to the Firebase Authentication UID (`request.auth.uid`).

Stores user-specific profile details (e.g., display name, avatar URL). This is a 1:1 relationship with UserAccount, stored as a single document named 'profile' within the subcollection. Includes denormalized 'userAccountId' field for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (Firebase Authentication UID).

definition.description: Stores individual user preferences and configurations for Nebulabs WebOS. Includes denormalized 'userAccountId' field for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (Firebase Authentication UID).

params.name: settingId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific user setting.

definition.description: Defines an application available in the Nebulabs OS App Store. This collection is globally readable.

params.name: applicationId
params.description: The unique identifier for an application.

definition.description: Records applications installed by a specific user, including user-specific preferences like pinning status and desktop position. Includes denormalized 'userAccountId' field for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (Firebase Authentication UID).

params.name: installedApplicationId
params.description: The unique identifier for a user's installed application record.

definition.description: Represents a folder within a user's virtual file system. The 'parentFolderId' references another folder within the same user's collection, maintaining ownership. Includes denormalized 'userAccountId' field for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (Firebase Authentication UID).

params.name: folderId
params.description: The unique identifier for a user's folder.

definition.description: Represents a file stored within a user's virtual file system. The 'folderId' references a folder within the same user's collection, maintaining ownership. Includes denormalized 'userAccountId' field for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (Firebase Authentication UID).

params.name: fileId
params.description: The unique identifier for a user's file.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for Nebulabs WebOS prioritizes robust security, scalability, and debuggability by adhering strictly to the core design principles. All user-specific data is organized hierarchically under a top-level `/users/{userId}` collection, where `{userId}` directly corresponds to `request.auth.uid`. This path-based ownership is fundamental for secure data access.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**
Authorization independence is achieved through pervasive denormalization. For every document residing in a user's subcollection (e.g., `profile`, `settings`, `installedApplications`, `folders`, `files`), the `userAccountId` field within the document itself is mandated to match the `{userId}` wildcard in its path. For example, a `UserSetting` document at `/users/{userId}/settings/{settingId}` will contain `userAccountId: '{userId}'`. This design eliminates the need for `get()` calls in Firestore Security Rules to check parent documents for ownership, which would otherwise break atomic operations and introduce performance overhead. Rules can directly compare `request.auth.uid` with `resource.data.userAccountId` or `request.path[1]` (which is the `{userId}` wildcard), ensuring efficient, atomic, and easily auditable access control.

**Querying for Authorized Paths (QAPs - Rules are not Filters):**
QAPs are inherently supported through **Structural Segregation**. The application's data is divided into two primary security postures:
1.  **Global Public Data:** The `/applications/{applicationId}` collection stores global application definitions. This collection has a homogeneous security posture (globally readable), allowing any authenticated (or even unauthenticated) user to list and retrieve application details without requiring complex filtering rules. A `list` operation on `/applications` will return all available apps.
2.  **User-Owned Private Data:** All subcollections under `/users/{userId}` (`profile`, `settings`, `installedApplications`, `folders`, `files`) are strictly private to the user identified by `{userId}`. When a user queries a `list` from, for example, `/users/{request.auth.uid}/settings`, the security rules can efficiently confirm that `request.auth.uid` matches the `{userId}` in the path, thereby guaranteeing that only the requesting user's data is returned. Any attempt to `list` documents from another user's path (e.g., `/users/anotherUserUid/settings`) will be explicitly denied by the rules, enforcing ownership structurally rather than by filtering results. This ensures that client-side list queries are secure by design, preventing data leakage and minimizing the burden on rules to filter unauthorized content.

**Summary of Design Principles Applied:**
*   **Authorization Independence:** Denormalized `userAccountId` in all user-owned documents.
*   **Clarity of Intent:** Paths clearly indicate ownership (`/users/{userId}/...`) and global scope (`/applications`).
*   **DBAC (No Custom Claims):** `request.auth.uid` is the sole determinant for user-specific data access.
*   **QAPs:** Achieved via structural segregation of global and user-owned data, with path-based authorization for user data.
*   **Invariants:** Ownership (`userAccountId`), timestamps (`createdAt`, `updatedAt`), and denormalized data (`isPinnedToTaskbar`, icon positions) are maintained within the respective documents.
